help.title: Quick help
help.description: A fast guide for first-time users. Pick the section that matches where you are in the flow.
help.footer: Layak gives draft guidance only. You still review and submit manually.

overview.title: How Layak works
overview.body: Layak turns one evaluation into a guided flow: understand your profile, match schemes, estimate upside, then prepare draft packets for you to review.
overview.point1: Start with sample data if you want a no-risk walkthrough first.
overview.point2: Upload or type your details, then wait for the pipeline to finish.
overview.point3: Use the results page to review schemes and download your draft packet.

documents.title: What documents to prepare
documents.body: The upload path works best when your files are recent, readable, and cropped to the important details.
documents.point1: Use the front of your IC, your latest payslip, and your latest utility bill.
documents.point2: Make sure names, dates, addresses, and RM amounts are easy to read.
documents.point3: If uploads feel troublesome, switch to the manual path instead.

samples.title: Try sample data first
samples.body: The sample personas are the safest way to understand the demo before touching real documents.
samples.point1: Aisyah shows the gig-worker / Form B path.
samples.point2: Farhan shows the salaried / Form BE path.
samples.point3: Both demos are synthetic and safe to explore.

results.title: What happens after results
results.body: The results page is where you decide what to do next: review matched schemes, inspect the draft packet, or start another evaluation.
results.point1: Check why each scheme matched before you act on it.
results.point2: Download the draft packet and review it carefully.
results.point3: Layak never submits for you — final submission is always manual.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.description: Start tracking your welfare eligibility for free. Upgrade to Pro when you need more power.

freeTier.name: Free
features: Access to STR, JKM, and LHDN schemes

hero.headlinePart2: you qualify for — in one upload.
hero.description: Layak reads your MyKad, payslip, and utility bill, then runs a five-step agent pipeline to match you against government schemes — STR, JKM Warga Emas, LHDN Form B reliefs, and more. Every number cites a source page.

cta.headline: Ready to see what you qualify for?
cta.description: Sign in with Google is one click — no account setup needed. Three uploads, a ranked list, three draft packets. Takes under a minute.

features.sectionDescription: Five steps. Every step streams to the UI in real time so you can see the reasoning as it happens.

step1.title: Extract
step1.description: Gemini Vision reads your three documents and pulls structured profile fields.

step2.title: Classify
step2.description: Household size, per-capita income, filer category, dependants are derived.

step3.title: Match
step3.description: Rule engine checks every profile against scheme thresholds with full citations.

step4.title: Compute
step4.description: Gemini Code Execution sums annual upside across qualifying schemes on stage.

step5.title: Generate
step5.description: WeasyPrint drafts three watermarked PDF packets — ready to review and submit.

howItWorks.step1Body: Gemini Vision reads your MyKad, payslip, and utility bill and pulls structured profile fields — name, IC, monthly income, household composition, address.
howItWorks.step2Body: Household size, per-capita monthly income, filer category (Form B vs BE), and counts of children under 18 and elderly dependants 60+ are derived from the extracted profile.
howItWorks.step3Body: The rule engine checks your derived profile against every scheme threshold — STR 2026 tiers, JKM Warga Emas eligibility, five LHDN Form B reliefs — and returns qualifying schemes with exact citations.
howItWorks.step4Body: Gemini writes a Python snippet and runs it in Google's code sandbox to sum your annual upside across every qualifying scheme. The generated code and its output stream live to the UI, so you see the full working — not just a final number.
howItWorks.step5Body: WeasyPrint renders three draft PDFs — BK-01 for STR, JKM18 for Warga Emas, a Form B relief summary for LHDN. Every page carries the watermark: DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED.
howItWorks.stackGeminiRole: Orchestrates the agent pipeline and handles document understanding + classification.
howItWorks.stackVertexRole: RAG layer over committed scheme PDFs — the source of every cited rule passage.
howItWorks.stackCodeExecRole: Gemini writes and runs a Python snippet in Google's sandbox to compute your annual upside. Both the generated source and its stdout stream to the UI — verifiable math, not a black box.
howItWorks.stackWeasyRole: HTML-to-PDF draft packet generator. Every page watermarked and agency-portal-ready.
howItWorks.finePrintDraft: Layak never submits on your behalf. Every generated packet is a draft — you review it and lodge it manually via the agency's official portal.
howItWorks.finePrintEstimates: Figures are estimates. The agency makes the final determination on every application.
howItWorks.finePrintIndependent: Layak is an independent prototype. It is not affiliated with LHDN, JKM, the Treasury, or any other Malaysian government body.
howItWorks.finePrintCoverage: Scheme coverage and thresholds reflect YA2025 / 2026 rules as of April 2026 and may change over time.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.intro: Layak is a hackathon submission to the MyAI Future Hackathon. This notice describes what we collect, what we do with it, and the rights you keep under Malaysia's Personal Data Protection Act 2010 (PDPA).
privacy.section1Google: Google account profile when you sign in: email, display name, profile photo, and the Firebase user identifier.
privacy.section1Documents: Documents you upload to run an evaluation — typically a MyKad, a payslip or income screenshot, and a utility bill.
privacy.section1History: Evaluation history: the structured profile we extracted from your documents and the schemes you matched against, persisted in Firestore so you can see your past runs.
privacy.section1Consent: Consent timestamp recorded when you tick the PDPA checkbox on sign-up.
privacy.section2MyKad: Your full MyKad number — we keep only the last four digits.
privacy.section2Files: The original bytes of your uploaded files. Extraction runs in-memory inside a single request, and the raw files are discarded once the structured profile is produced.
privacy.section2Logs: IC numbers, authentication tokens, or document content in any log line.
privacy.section3Matching: To match you against locked Malaysian schemes (STR 2026, JKM Warga Emas, LHDN Form B reliefs).
privacy.section3Packets: To generate your draft application packets, every page watermarked "DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED."
privacy.section3History: To show you a history of your past evaluations on the dashboard.
privacy.section3FairUse: To enforce per-user fair-use rate limits on the free tier.
privacy.section4Body: All processing and storage happens in Google Cloud, asia-southeast1 region. The Cloud Run services and the Firestore database are owned by the Layak team. Authentication is handled by Firebase Authentication, also under our project.
privacy.section5FreeDelete: Free-tier evaluations are deleted after 30 days by an automated nightly job.
privacy.section5ProKeep: Pro-tier evaluations persist until you delete them.
privacy.section5AccountDelete: You can request immediate deletion of your account and all linked records at any time from the Settings page.
privacy.section6Access: Access — download a JSON export of your profile and full evaluation history.
privacy.section6Deletion: Deletion — remove your account, your Firestore records, and your Firebase Auth identity in one action.
privacy.section6Withdrawal: Withdrawal — revoke consent by deleting your account. Further sign-in attempts will require fresh consent.
privacy.section7NoSubmit: We never submit anything on your behalf to LHDN, JPN, JKM, or any government portal. Every output is a draft packet you submit manually.
privacy.section7NoThirdParty: We never share your data with third parties for advertising.
privacy.section7NoSell: We do not sell your data.
privacy.section8Body: Layak is a non-production hackathon project. Use synthetic documents wherever possible during the demo period. Do not upload another person's MyKad or financial documents without their explicit consent.
privacy.section9Body: Reach the team via the project's GitHub repository. For PDPA-specific queries, open an issue tagged pdpa.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: Layak is a research demonstration built for the MyAI Future Hackathon. By using it, you agree to the terms below.
terms.section1Body: Layak is a demonstration system, not a licensed financial, tax, or legal adviser. Outputs are educational and may be incomplete or out of date. Always confirm eligibility, amounts, and deadlines with the relevant agency (LHDN, JPN, JKM, or your local PPM) before acting on anything Layak shows you.
terms.section2Body: Every application packet Layak generates is watermarked "DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED." Layak does not, and will not, file anything on your behalf with any Malaysian government portal. Submission is always manual and always your responsibility.
terms.section3OwnAccount: Sign in with your own Google account.
terms.section3OwnDocuments: Upload documents that belong to you, or for which you have explicit consent.
terms.section3NoEvade: Do not use Layak to evade taxes, misrepresent eligibility, or apply for schemes you do not qualify for.
terms.section4Body: We may suspend or delete demo accounts that violate these terms or that abuse the free-tier rate limit (5 evaluations per rolling 24 hours).
terms.section5Body: Layak is provided "as is," without warranty of any kind. The team makes no representation that the service will be available, accurate, or fit for any specific purpose during or after the hackathon judging window.
terms.section6Body: To the extent permitted by Malaysian law, the Layak team is not liable for any direct, indirect, or consequential loss arising from your use of the service. You remain responsible for the accuracy of your own application packets and for any decisions you take based on Layak's output.
terms.section7Body: We may update these terms during the hackathon period. The effective date at the top of this page reflects the latest version.
terms.section8Body: These terms are governed by the laws of Malaysia.
terms.section9Body: Reach the team via the project's GitHub repository.

signIn.title: Welcome back
signIn.description: Continue with your Google account to reach your dashboard.
signIn.guestNotice: Shared demo account — please don't enter real personal information.
signIn.errorGuestFailed: Guest sign-in failed. Please try again.

signUp.title: Create your account
signUp.description: Sign up with Google. Layak never submits anything on your behalf — you stay in control of every draft packet.
signUp.consentPrefix: I consent to Layak processing my uploaded documents (IC, payslip, utility bill) to match government schemes, per the

hero.description: Your civic profile is up to date. Layak reviews your current situation against the latest 2026 government schemes — STR, JKM Warga Emas, LHDN Form B reliefs. Takes about 4 minutes.

activeApplications.title: Application drafts ready
activeApplications.emptyDescription: When an evaluation completes, its draft application packets land here so you can lodge them with the agencies.

recentActivity.title: Recent Activity
recentActivity.emptyDescription: Evaluations stream here as they happen.

overview.title: Your eligibility at a glance.
overview.description: Your highest discovered relief and a paginated history of past evaluations.

results.title: Based on your documents, you qualify for multiple relief schemes.
results.description: Every number cites a source page; every packet is a DRAFT you submit yourself.
results.notFoundBody: We couldn't find this evaluation. It may have been deleted, or the link may be wrong.
results.byIdDescription: Hydrated from your saved evaluation. Numbers cite the rule and source page; every packet is a DRAFT you submit yourself.
results.deadlineTitle: Draft only — check deadlines before you submit
results.deadlineBody: Layak never files for you. Review the matched schemes, confirm the latest official deadline on the agency side, then submit manually through the correct portal.

actions.title: Take the next step with confidence.
actions.description: Start with the most important action, then work through the rest in order.
actions.reviewMatchesBody: See why each scheme matched before you act on anything.
actions.reviewMatchesEmpty: No qualifying schemes surfaced in this run.
actions.reviewPacketTitle: Review or Download Your Draft Packet
actions.reviewPacketBody: Open the packet section next so you can inspect or download the draft documents.
actions.reviewPacketEmpty: A draft packet appears here only when a scheme qualifies.
actions.startAnotherBody: Run a fresh evaluation if you want to compare another profile or document set.

intake.quickStartTitle: Choose the easiest way to begin.
intake.quickStartDescription: Try sample data for the fastest walkthrough, upload your real documents if they are ready, or type the details yourself if that feels easier.
intake.sampleDescription: Best for demos, judges, and first-time visitors who want to see the full flow before using real documents.
intake.sampleFooter: Aisyah = gig / Form B · Farhan = salaried / Form BE
intake.uploadDescription: Best when you already have a clear IC, latest payslip, and latest utility bill ready to go.
intake.uploadFooter: Three files in, one guided evaluation out
intake.manualDescription: Best when you do not have the files nearby or want a simpler no-upload path.
intake.manualFooter: Good fallback when uploads feel inconvenient

upload.hintIc: Front of your Malaysian identity card, clearly showing your details.
upload.hintPayslip: Latest month if available; self-employed filers can upload a bank statement or LHDN form.
upload.hintUtility: Water, electricity, or broadband bill for address verification — within the last three months.
upload.errorFileSize: File is {{size}} MB — max is 10 MB.
upload.errorFileType: Unsupported file type ({{type}}). Upload an image or PDF.
upload.dropzoneFormats: JPG, PNG, or PDF up to 10MB
upload.householdHint: Documents don't list your dependants. Add children / parents / other household members here so schemes that gate on them (JKM Warga Emas, LHDN child relief) surface.
upload.readyIc: Use the front of your IC and make sure the text is easy to read.
upload.readyPayslip: Use your latest payslip or income statement.
upload.readyUtility: Use your latest electricity, water, or broadband bill.
upload.pageDescription: Choose the easiest way to start: try sample data, upload your documents, or type the details manually. Layak turns that into draft guidance you still review and submit yourself.
upload.householdCollapsed: Skip this unless you want to add children, parents, or other family members.
upload.householdAdded: {{count}} household member added. Open to review or edit.
upload.samplesHint: Use Aisyah for the gig-worker path or Farhan for the salaried path. Both are synthetic demos.
upload.trustCopy: Layak creates draft guidance only. You review the results and submit manually through the official agency portal.

manual.dobHelp: Just type the numbers — we fill in the dashes. Or use the calendar icon.
manual.icHelp: We only collect the last 4 digits — your full MyKad never leaves your device.
manual.incomeHelp: Net payout for gig work, basic pay for salaried.
manual.employmentGigHelp: Grab, Foodpanda, freelance — files LHDN Form B.
manual.employmentSalariedHelp: Regular monthly salary — files LHDN Form BE.
manual.costHelp: The RM amount on the bottom of your latest TNB bill. Most people remember this more easily than kWh.
manual.kwhHelp: Optional — only if your bill lists "Jumlah Penggunaan".

dependants.description: Add everyone else who shares your household. Children under 18 and parents aged 60+ unlock specific schemes. Household size: {{size}} (you + {{count}} {{plural}}).
dependants.empty: No dependants yet. Click "Add dependant" to list each household member.

demo.title: DEMO MODE
demo.description: Running against Aisyah — a synthetic Grab driver in Kuantan, two school-age children and one elderly dependant. No real MyKad is involved.
demo.description_aisyah: Running against Aisyah — a synthetic Grab driver in Kuantan, two school-age children and one elderly dependant. No real MyKad is involved.
demo.description_farhan: Running against Cikgu Farhan — a synthetic salaried teacher in Subang Jaya, spouse and two school-age children. Files Form BE. No real MyKad is involved.

error.title: Pipeline error
error.recoveryIntro: Something broke mid-pipeline. You can run the full flow against synthetic Aisyah documents — useful for confirming the rest of the UI is healthy — or start over with fresh uploads.

quotaExhausted.title: Daily Quota Exhausted
quotaExhausted.body: We've run the day's Gemini OCR quota. Switch to Manual Entry — the rest of the pipeline runs exactly the same way, just without document scanning.

serviceUnavailable.title: Gemini Is Temporarily Unavailable
serviceUnavailable.body: This usually clears in under a minute. Retry the same evaluation, or fall back to the bundled Aisyah sample while the upstream recovers.

deadlineExceeded.title: Upstream Request Timed Out
deadlineExceeded.body: The Gemini call took longer than we allow. Retry with the same documents, or switch to Manual Entry to skip the OCR step entirely.

permissionDenied.title: Gemini Access Not Authorised
permissionDenied.body: The server couldn't authenticate against Gemini for this request. Open Settings to confirm you're signed in on the right tier; the on-call team has been notified.

extractValidation.title: Couldn't Read the Documents Cleanly
extractValidation.body: We extracted text but the result didn't fit our profile schema. Try Manual Entry (you'll type the fields yourself) or drop in clearer scans via the sample pack.

generic.title: Pipeline Error
generic.body: Something unexpected happened mid-pipeline. Run the full flow against synthetic Aisyah documents to check the rest of the UI, or start over with fresh uploads.

upside.label: Total Potential Relief
upside.empty: Run your first evaluation to see your total potential annual relief across federal and state schemes.
upside.description: Matched across {{count}} scheme(s). Total shown here includes upside schemes only, based on your income profile and household status.

schemeCard.heading: Eligible Schemes
schemeCard.noMatches: No qualifying schemes found for your profile in this build.
schemeCard.emptyList: Scheme matches will appear here after your first evaluation completes.

requiredContributions.heading: Required Contributions
requiredContributions.intro: Mandatory contributions you owe under these schemes. These are NOT included in your annual relief total above — they're money you pay.
requiredContributions.footnote: Register and pay directly via the agency portal. Non-payment may affect access to employment-injury benefits if you claim later.

preview.title: Inline preview ({{count}} drafts)
preview.description: Inspect each draft PDF in the browser before downloading. Each preview re-runs WeasyPrint server-side from your stored profile + matches; the bytes never persist.

packet.description: Every page carries a "DRAFT — NOT SUBMITTED" watermark. Layak never submits on your behalf. Review each PDF, then lodge it yourself via the agency's official portal.
packet.placeholderNotice: WeasyPrint packet generator lands in Phase 1 Task 5 (paired wiring block). Until then the packet shell is rendered so you can see where the download CTAs will sit.
packet.notificationBody: Your draft files are ready on this device. Review them carefully before manual submission.

codeExecution.title: Gemini Code Execution
codeExecution.description: Optional technical trace for the annual upside math. Expand it if you want to inspect the generated Python and stdout.

schemes.pageTitle: Every Malaysian scheme Layak reasons over.
schemes.pageDescription: Six federal schemes are live in this build. Three more land in v2 as we wire each one's rules, thresholds, and application form into the engine.

howWePick.title: How We Pick What to Support
howWePick.description: Layak prioritises schemes with publicly-documented thresholds, open application portals, and high real-world upside for low-to-middle-income Malaysians. Each rule we support is backed by a citable source — either a government PR document, gazetted form, or scheme brochure — so every RM figure in your evaluation traces back to a specific page.

schemes.notAffiliated: Not affiliated with LHDN, JKM, Treasury, or any other Malaysian government body.

str.summary: Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah — household and individual tiers based on per-capita income and household size.

jkm.summary: Monthly RM 600 payment for a qualifying elderly dependant (aged 60+) in low-income households.

jkmBkk.summary: RM 100/month per child (capped RM 450/month per household) for low-income families with school-age children.

lhdn.summary: Five tax reliefs covering both Form B (self-employed) and Form BE (salaried) filers: individual, child, parent medical, EPF + life insurance, and lifestyle.

perkesoSksps.summary: Self-employed social-security contribution (Akta 789). Mandatory for gig and passenger-transport workers — buys medical, disability, and survivor cover.

iSaraan.summary: Government tops up 15% of self-employed voluntary EPF contribution, capped RM 500/yr. Targets Form B filers aged 18-60 without employer EPF.

settings.title: Your account and data.
settings.description: Profile, tier, and the PDPA controls for your data.

tier.title: Plan
tier.freeHint: Free tier caps evaluations to a rolling 24-hour window and prunes history after 30 days.

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.description: Export or remove the data Layak holds about you.
danger.exportDescription: Downloads a JSON bundle of your account profile and every evaluation, per PDPA §26.
danger.deleteDescription: Removes every evaluation and your account record, then revokes Firebase Auth, per PDPA §34. This cannot be undone.
danger.deleteConfirm: This permanently deletes your account, every evaluation, and your sign-in record. Continue?
danger.guestLockedTitle: Account deletion disabled for guest demo
danger.guestLockedDescription: You're signed in as the shared demo account. Account-wide deletion is locked so other judges keep their session. Sign out instead — your individual evaluations stay deletable from the history table.

upgrade.title: Free tier reached
upgrade.descriptionDefault: You've hit the free-tier evaluation cap. The Pro tier is a placeholder narrative in v2 — there is no real subscription product yet.
upgrade.descriptionRateLimit: You've hit the free-tier cap of {{limit}} evaluations every {{hours}} hours. The cap resets automatically — no signup required.
upgrade.feature2: History persists indefinitely (free tier auto-prunes after 30 days).
upgrade.feature3: Priority access to new schemes as they're added.
